title: Is Juicing Really Healthy?
subtitle: 榨汁真的对你有好处吗

paragraphs.en: Freshly juiced fruit has become a basic dietary item — especially for busy, health-conscious consumers who can save time on making (and perhaps even chewing) their food without missing out on nutrients. Fruit juice also has been linked to claims that it can help you both lose weight and stay healthy. All of which, of course, has made it highly profitable. The global fruit and vegetable juices market was valued at $154 billion in 2016 and is expected to grow. But is juicing really as healthy as we think?
paragraphs.zh: 鲜榨果汁已成为基本饮食项目——尤其对于忙碌且注重健康的消费者而言，既省去了制作（甚至咀嚼）食物的时间，又不会错过营养。果汁还被宣称有助于减肥和保持健康。这一切自然使其利润丰厚。2016年全球果蔬汁市场估值达1540亿美元，预计还将增长。但榨汁真如我们想的那么健康吗？

words.meaning: adj. 盈利的，有利可图的

paragraphs.en: Most foods containing fructose — a naturally occurring sugar found in all fruits and fruit juices — don't seem to have harmful effects as long as they're not contributing to excess daily calories. This is because the fibre found in whole fruits is intact, and the sugar is contained within the fruit's cells. It takes our digestive system a while to break these cells down and for fructose to enter the bloodstream. But this is not the case with fruit juice. Unlike whole fruit, fruit juice has most of the fibre removed. This is why the fructose in fruit juices counts as free sugars — which also include honey and the sugars added to foods.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数含果糖的食物——果糖是存在于所有水果和果汁中的天然糖分——只要不导致每日热量超标，似乎并无害处。这是因为完整水果中的纤维保持完整，糖分被包裹在果肉细胞内。我们的消化系统需要一定时间才能分解这些细胞，让果糖进入血液。但果汁则不然。与完整水果不同，果汁去除了大部分纤维。正因如此，果汁中的果糖被归为“游离糖”——蜂蜜和添加到食物中的糖也属此类。

paragraphs.en: It's recommended by the World Health Organisation that adults have no more than 30g of added sugar, the equivalent of 150ml of fruit juice, per day. The problem is that, with the fibre removed, fruit juice's fructose is absorbed more quickly. Sudden increases in blood sugar cause the release of insulin to bring it back down to a stable level. Over time, this mechanism can wear out, increasing the risk of developing type 2 diabetes.
paragraphs.zh: 世界卫生组织建议成年人每日添加糖摄入量不超过30克，相当于150毫升果汁。问题在于，去除纤维后，果汁中的果糖吸收更快。血糖骤升会促使胰岛素释放，将血糖拉回稳定水平。久而久之，这一机制可能失灵，增加患2型糖尿病的风险。

paragraphs.en: In 2013, researchers analysed the health data of 100,000 people collected between 1986 and 2009 and found that fruit juice consumption was linked with increased risk of type 2 diabetes. Another study found a relationship between fruit juice and type 2 diabetes after following the diets and diabetes status of more than 70,000 nurses over 18 years. The researchers explain that the possible reason for this may partly have been the lack of the other components found in whole fruit, like fibre. Juices containing vegetables can provide more nutrients and less sugar than juices made up of just fruit — but they still lack valuable fibre. Diets high in fibre have been linked to a lower risk of developing heart disease, stroke, high blood pressure and diabetes, and it's recommended adults consume 30g per day.
paragraphs.zh: 2013年，研究人员分析了1986至2009年间收集的10万人健康数据，发现饮用果汁与2型糖尿病风险增加有关。另一项研究追踪了7万多名护士18年间的饮食和糖尿病状况，同样发现果汁与2型糖尿病存在关联。研究人员解释，可能的部分原因是缺乏完整水果中的其他成分，如纤维。含蔬菜的果汁比纯果汁营养更丰富、糖分更少，但仍缺乏宝贵的纤维。高纤维饮食与降低心脏病、中风、高血压和糖尿病风险相关，建议成年人每日摄入30克纤维。

paragraphs.en: Aside from the link to type 2 diabetes, many studies agree that fruit juice is harmful if it contributes to an excess in calories. In an analysis of 155 studies, John Sievenpiper, associate professor at the University of Toronto's Department of Nutritional Sciences, set out to understand if the associations between sugary soft drinks and health — including risk of diabetes and heart disease — were applicable to the food and drink we generally consume as part of a healthy diet. He compared studies looking into the effects of fructose-containing sugars with control diets free from or lower in these sugars. His aim? To isolate the effects of consuming too many calories from the effects of foods containing different sugars.
paragraphs.zh: 除与2型糖尿病有关外，许多研究一致认为，如果果汁导致热量过剩，则有害健康。多伦多大学营养科学系副教授约翰·西文派珀分析了155项研究，旨在弄清含糖软饮料与健康（包括糖尿病和心脏病风险）之间的关联是否适用于我们通常作为健康饮食一部分的食品饮料。他将研究含果糖食物影响的研究与不含或含较少此类糖分的对照饮食进行了比较。目的何在？将摄入过量热量的影响与含不同糖分食物的影响分离开来。

paragraphs.en: He found negative effects on blood sugar and insulin levels when food provided excess calories from sugars, including fruit juice. However, when the calories weren't in excess overall, there were some advantages to consuming whole fruit — and even fruit juice. Sievenpiper concludes that the recommended 150ml of fruit juice per day, which is an average serving, is reasonable. The paper concluded that foods containing fructose may have some small benefits for long-term blood sugar control when they don't lead to over-consumption of calories. But when they do lead to too many calories, they risk raising blood sugar and insulin levels.
paragraphs.zh: 他发现，当食物（包括果汁）中的糖分导致热量过剩时，会对血糖和胰岛素水平产生负面影响。然而，当总体热量不超标时，食用完整水果——甚至果汁——则有一些好处。西文派珀总结道，推荐的每日150毫升果汁（平均一份）是合理的。论文结论指出，在不导致热量超标的前提下，含果糖的食物可能对长期血糖控制有微小益处。但若导致热量超标，则有升高血糖和胰岛素水平的风险。

words.meaning: n. 过度消费，摄入过多

paragraphs.en: So while we know that fruit juice can cause diabetes if it's part of a diet that has more calories overall, it's less clear how juice affects the long-term health of those who aren't overweight. But we have a higher risk of consuming more than the recommended daily number of calories (around 2,000 for women and 2,500 for men) on the days we drink juice, according to research. Numerous studies have shown that drinking fruit juice doesn't make us consume any less of other foods throughout the day. As it is easy to consume large amounts of fruit juice quite quickly, extra calories are taken in. And extra calories can contribute to weight gain.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，虽然我们知道如果果汁是高热量饮食的一部分，可能导致糖尿病，但尚不清楚果汁如何影响非超重者的长期健康。不过研究表明，在喝果汁的日子里，我们摄入的热量超过每日推荐值（女性约2000卡，男性约2500卡）的风险更高。大量研究显示，喝果汁并不会让我们一天中少吃其他食物。由于很容易快速摄入大量果汁，多余热量随之而来，而多余热量可能导致体重增加。

paragraphs.en: One way we can improve the effects of fruit juice on our health is making sure we select ripe fruit to retain as much of its goodness as possible, according to Roger Clemens, professor of pharmaceutical sciences at the University of Southern California. Another reason for fruit juice's recent popularity is the argument that it can help to remove poisonous or harmful substances from the body. However, 'the whole concept of a juice diet helping to remove poisonous or harmful substances from the body is completely false,' says Clemens. 'We consume compounds every day that may be poisonous and our bodies do a wonderful job of removing and eliminating anything harmful from our bodies.'
paragraphs.zh: 南加州大学药物科学教授罗杰·克莱门斯表示，改善果汁健康效应的方法之一是确保选用成熟水果，以尽可能保留其营养成分。果汁近期流行的另一个理由是声称它能帮助排出体内有毒有害物质。然而克莱门斯说：“果汁饮食有助于排毒这一整套概念完全是错误的。我们每天摄入的可能有毒的化合物，身体都能出色地将其清除和排出。”

paragraphs.en: And juice isn't a cure-all for getting your nutrients, either. 'There are a lot of nutrients contained in the parts of the fruits, such as in apple skins, that are excluded when you juice them,' says Heather Ferris, a professor of medicine. 'You end up with sugary water with some vitamins.' Plus, she adds, it's not an ideal way to reach the advised five a day. 'People try to get five servings of fruit and vegetables per day and don't realise this isn't just about getting vitamins,' she says. 'It's also about reducing the amount of grain carbs and protein and fats in our diet, and increasing fibre.' So while fruit juice is better than no fruit at all, there are limits. It particularly comes with risks when we consume more than 150ml of free sugars per day, or when it puts us over our recommended calorie intake. It provides us with vitamins — but fruit juice is far from a quick fix.
paragraphs.zh: 而且果汁也并非获取营养的万能灵药。医学教授希瑟·费里斯说：“水果的某些部分含有大量营养，比如苹果皮，榨汁时却被丢弃了。你最终得到的只是含些维生素的糖水。”她补充道，这也不是实现“每日五份”建议的理想方式。“人们努力每天吃五份蔬果，却没意识到这不仅关乎维生素摄入，还关乎减少饮食中的谷物碳水、蛋白质和脂肪，并增加纤维。”因此，虽然果汁聊胜于无，但有其局限。当每日游离糖摄入超过150毫升，或超过推荐热量摄入时，它尤其具有风险。它提供维生素——但果汁远非速效良方。
